description:
Offered: as needed
Independent study is a student-led academic experience that may take the form of problem identification and research, special advanced reading programs and/or a scientific literature review. Independent study cannot be substituted for regularly scheduled courses. Students are expected to complete or conduct a scientific literature review as part of this experience. Independent study may be taken for one to six semester hours.
Prerequisite(s): 3.0 cumulative average
Note: This experience is offered primarily to Psychological Science majors or minors, or Neuroscience, Behavior and Cognition minors, of junior or senior standing, and is arranged by mutual agreement with a supervising professor.
